functional_requirements.title: Create Order
functional_requirements.description: User creates an order with user ID and item list. Checks user existence, product stock, calculates total price, deducts stock, and creates order record with status pending.

functional_requirements.title: Pay Order
functional_requirements.description: User pays for an order. Checks order exists and status is pending, checks user balance, deducts balance, updates order status to paid.

functional_requirements.title: Ship Order
functional_requirements.description: Ship an order. Checks order exists and status is paid, updates status to shipped.

functional_requirements.title: Complete Order
functional_requirements.description: Complete an order. Checks order exists and status is shipped, updates status to completed.

functional_requirements.title: Cancel Order
functional_requirements.description: Cancel an order. Checks order exists and status is pending or paid, restores product stock, refunds balance if paid, updates status to cancelled.

functional_requirements.title: List Orders
functional_requirements.description: List orders with optional filters by user and status. Displays order details including items, total price, status, and time.

functional_requirements.title: Get User Orders
functional_requirements.description: Get all orders for a specific user. Calculates total spent and counts of orders by status.

functional_requirements.title: List Products
functional_requirements.description: List products with info and stock. Can filter low stock items.

non_functional_requirements.description: Balance and stock must be consistent across operations (deduction and restoration).

acceptance_criteria.description: Create order successfully with valid user and sufficient stock.
Create order fails with insufficient stock.
Pay order successfully with sufficient balance.
Pay order fails with insufficient balance.
Cancel paid order restores stock and refunds balance.
Order status transitions correctly: pending -> paid -> shipped -> completed.

storage.details: Three independent in-memory data sources: users, products, orders.

glossary.order: A record of a purchase transaction containing user, items, total price, status, and creation time.
glossary.pending: Initial order status after creation, awaiting payment.
glossary.completed: Final order status after delivery completion.
glossary.cancelled: Order status after cancellation, with possible resource restoration.

input_spec.description: Input is a JSON object with command and order_data fields, passed to manage_order function.

schema.command: string (one of: create_order, pay_order, ship_order, complete_order, cancel_order, list_orders, get_user_orders, list_products)
schema.order_data: object containing optional fields: user_id (int), order_id (int), items (list of {product_id: int, quantity: int}), status_filter (string), user_filter (int), low_stock (bool)

output_spec.description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.

schema.data: object containing relevant data (e.g., order_id, total_price, products list, etc.)
